Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

> Thanks for review. The issue in lfsck_layout_scan_orphan I solved by changing the order of oi_id and oi_seq in ost_id struct. Please see next version.

You can NOT change the ost_id members order. It is an union, changing order means different mapping with oi_fid members. That may cause kinds of FID <=> OSTID covertion issues.

Patch Set 3:

> > Thanks for review. The issue in lfsck_layout_scan_orphan I solved
 > by changing the order of oi_id and oi_seq in ost_id struct. Please
 > see next version.
 > 
 > You can NOT change the ost_id members order. It is an union,
 > changing order means different mapping with oi_fid members. That
 > may cause kinds of FID <=> OSTID conversion issues.
Fan Yong, wasn't it a mistake to map oi_fid.f_seq -> oi.oi_id and (oi_fid.f_id, oi_fid.f_ver) -> oi.oi_seq? the patch just fixes it.

Patch Set 3:

> Fan Yong, wasn't it a mistake to map oi_fid.f_seq -> oi.oi_id and (oi_fid.f_id, oi_fid.f_ver) -> oi.oi_seq? the patch just fixes it.

I have to say that the current implementation is confused because of the historical problem. Even if without considering compatibility/interoperability issues, we still need to update related conversion functions.

As you can see, there are many new Maloo test failures with your latest patch applied. Many of them should be related with your changing of oi_seq.

So, keep them there may be the best solution currently.

parents.subject: LU-9505 llapi: treat MDT index as a hex number

commit.subject: LU-9442 osp: can't create IDIF fid number > 0xFFFFFFFF
commit.message:
LU-9442 osp: can't create IDIF fid number > 0xFFFFFFFF

fid_is_last_id didn't recognize IDIFes. This caused OST
to allocate new sequence despite of MDT still used
initial FID_SEQ_IDIF. Finally allocation failed with -115
returned from osd_check_lma:
osd_check_lma()) lustre-OST0000-osd: FID [0x100000001:0x0:0x0] != self_fid [0x100000000:0x0:0x0]

Patch has several typical "IDIF" fixes. Also it has lov_objid
fix to store all 48 IDIF bits instead of 32.
Finally it changes union fields order in ost_id.
Before the fix oi_fid.f_seq addressed oi_id instead of oi_seq.

Change-Id: Ifbda97a5b228254aedcb050c3d94d2ecb3a9590c
Seagate-bug-id: MRP-4392
Signed-off-by: Sergey Cheremencev <sergey.cheremencev@seagate.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/27225
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Mike Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
